plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate high risk.
Trim QQQI and QQQ to reduce exposure.
Significantly trim SPY to address concentration.
Add RTX and AMAT for their strong scanner readings.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation.
Expect lower volatility and a modest return target.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, accepting lower potential gains for greater stability. Drawdowns should be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI and SPY to lower exposure and concentration.
Add CRWD to increase exposure to a strong performer.
Add AMAT and RTX for their favorable risk/reward.
This plan balances growth with risk management.
Expect moderate growth with controlled drawdowns.
balanced.read: This plan targets around 2.5% per month by balancing growth and risk. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding high-quality names. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase SOXL and add SPAL for leveraged growth.
Sell QQQI to free up capital for higher-beta assets.
Trim QQQ and SPY to reduce broad exposure.
Add CRWD and AMAT, focusing on top scanner setups.
This plan aggressively pursues growth.
Expect higher potential returns but also larger drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This plan aims for aggressive growth, targeting around 4% per month. It embraces leverage and concentration in high-conviction names, accepting significantly higher risk and potential drawdowns. Daily leverage decay is a factor.

plan: Sell all leveraged and high-beta single names.
Sell volatile sector ETFs like SMH.
Significantly increase QQQI for income generation.
Increase SPY for stability and broad market exposure.
Add XLV for defensive sector exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown.
income.read: This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income with a target of around 1% per month. It minimizes risk by cutting leverage and volatile assets, favoring income-generating and defensive assets. NAV can still fall in market downturns.
